other_observers: David Waltman, Tina Jones, Forrest Luke, Thomas and Christof Heinrich; don't know
specific_location: Pasture on E side of Larimer CR 25-E, about half a mile north of Rist Canyon Road
description: The bird was clearly a meadowlark, with the classic "football" meadowlark shape, the mustard-yellow breast with the black V, the cryptic brown upperparts, etcetera. However, the bird was strikingly different in two respects from the Western Meadowlarks I am used to: first, its head was striped black and white instead of dull brown and white; and second, its face was clean white, down to and including the malar. There are many borderline, hard-to-identify meadowlarks out there, but this was not one.Behaviors: The bird was not visible or audible for the first twenty minutes of our stay. Then it popped up on a fencepost across the field for us and sang for several minutes. It repeated one song type many times before switching, but switched song types at least four times before dropping back into the grass about ten minutes later.Call: Bird sang at least four different song types during our observation, but was not heard to call.Plumage: unknown
